originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&n` is never used.

presenceCondition:
(and (= CONFIG_LOCKDEP true)
     (= CONFIG_SLAB true)
     (= CONFIG_FORCE_MAX_ZONEORDER true)
     (= CONFIG_SLOB false)
     (= CONFIG_PPC_256K_PAGES true))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_SLAB () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SLOB () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_PPC_256K_PAGES () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_LOCKDEP () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_FORCE_MAX_ZONEORDER () Bool
  true)
reasoning: Line 41 (reported as L39): `int n = *cache` inside init_node_lock_keys() (compiled when LOCKDEP=T). n is assigned but never used after — dead store. kmalloc_caches is only declared when SLOB=F (L25-27); SLOB=T makes it undeclared (compile error at L36). Dead store requires LOCKDEP=T AND SLOB=F. No '// ERROR' at L41 → new bug not in VBDB. (ERROR at L36 is for the VBDB-known kmalloc_caches access bug.) PC adds SLAB=T, FORCE_MAX_ZONEORDER=T, PPC_256K_PAGES=T — these only affect KMALLOC_SHIFT_HIGH array size, not the dead store. PC is over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
